role: Chair of the Federal Reserve
note: Transition: Jerome H. Powell's term as Chair ended; the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh 54-45 on 2026-05-13 (NPR/CNBC) and he took the oath as Chair on 2026-05-22 (federalreserve.gov). Powell remains on the Board as a governor. Warsh is the 17th Fed Chair; four-year term runs to 2030.

role: Vice Chair of the Federal Reserve
note: Took office as Vice Chair on 2023-09-13 for a four-year term ending 2027-09-07; still serving as of 2026-06-29.

role: Vice Chair for Supervision of the Federal Reserve
Chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
Chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
note: Nominated 2025-10-27; confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 16th CFTC Chairman on 2025-12-22. Previously chief counsel of the SEC's Crypto Task Force. Caroline Pham had been acting chair earlier in 2025.

role: Chairman of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
note: Confirmed by the Senate 2025-12-18; sworn in as 23rd FDIC Chairman on 2026-01-13 for a five-year term. Had served as Acting Chairman from 2025-01-20 until confirmation.

role: Chairman of the National Credit Union Administration
note: Designated NCUA Chairman by President Trump in January 2025. His six-year board term expired in August 2025, but he is serving in holdover capacity as Chairman pending a confirmed successor (John Crews nominated). Two NCUA board seats remain vacant amid litigation over the April 2025 dismissals of Todd Harper and Tanya Otsuka.

role: Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
note: Contested leadership. Russell Vought (also OMB Director) has been CFPB Acting Director since Feb. 2025 and remained acting as of late June 2026, but federal law bars him from serving past 2026-08-01. Per a 2026-06-02 report, Chief Legal Officer Mark Paoletta was elevated to Deputy Director and is slated to become Acting Director on Vought's exit. Separately, on 2026-06-10 President Trump nominated Brian Johnson as permanent CFPB Director (his third nominee, after Jonathan McKernan and Stuart Levenbach).

role: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency
note: Senate-confirmed (March 2025); sworn in 2025-03-14 as 5th FHFA Director. In June 2026 Pulte was also named Acting Director of National Intelligence while retaining his FHFA directorship and chairmanship of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

role: Chair of the Federal Trade Commission
Chairman of the Federal Communications Commission
Chairman of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
note: Confirmed as Commissioner 2025-10-07 (term to 2030-06-30); designated Chairman 2025-10-23, succeeding acting chair David Rosner. Republican Mark Christie chaired FERC earlier in 2025 before departing.

role: Chairman of the National Labor Relations Board
note: After 2025 NLRB quorum/membership turmoil, Murphy was sworn in as a Board Member 2026-01-07 (term to 2027-12-16) and designated Chairman by President Trump on 2026-03-27, succeeding former Chairman Marvin Kaplan, to whom Murphy had been Chief Counsel.

role: Chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
note: Lucas (Commissioner since 2020) was named Acting Chair on 2025-01-20, reconfirmed by the Senate 2025-07-31 for a term to 2030-07-01, and formally designated Chair (announced 2025-11-06).

role: President and CEO of the Securities Investor Protection Corporation
note: Post became President and CEO on 2025-07-01, succeeding Josephine Wang, who retired 2025-06-30 after 41 years.

role: Chairman of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board
note: Appointed by the SEC (announced 2026-01-30) and sworn in 2026-02-10 amid the 2025-2026 PCAOB leadership overhaul; term expires 2030-10-24. Sworn in alongside new board members Mark Calabria and Steven Laughton.

role: President and Chairman of the Export-Import Bank of the United States
jurisdiction: Export-Import Bank of the United States
note: Confirmed by the U.S. Senate as President and Chairman (announced 2025-09-19); term expiring 2029-01-20.

role: Administrator of the Small Business Administration
note: 28th SBA Administrator; former U.S. Senator from Georgia. Confirmed by the Senate 2025-02-19.

role: Administrator of NASA
note: Trump nominated Isaacman in late 2024/early 2025; nomination withdrawn May 2025; re-nominated 2025-11-04. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy served as acting/interim administrator from July 2025 until Isaacman was confirmed by the Senate 67-30 on 2025-12-17 and sworn in (15th Administrator) 2025-12-18.

role: Chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
note: Nieh sworn in as NRC Commissioner 2025-12-04 (term ending 2029-06-30) and designated 20th Chairman by President Trump on 2026-01-08, replacing David Wright (designated chair 2025-01-21, remains a commissioner).

role: Commissioner of Social Security
note: Senate confirmed Bisignano 53-47 on 2025-05-06; sworn in as the 18th Senate-confirmed SSA Commissioner in May 2025 (SSA marked his one-year anniversary on 2026-05-07).

role: Administrator of the General Services Administration
note: Senate confirmed Forst 2025-12-18; sworn in as GSA Administrator 2025-12-24. Stephen Ehikian had served as acting administrator earlier in 2025.

role: Director of the Office of Personnel Management
note: Senate confirmed Kupor 49-46 on 2025-07-09; sworn in as OPM Director 2025-07-14.

role: Acting Director of the National Science Foundation
note: Director Sethuraman Panchanathan resigned effective 2025-04-24; chief of staff Brian Stone has performed the duties of director since. President Trump nominated Jim (James) O'Neill as NSF Director on 2026-03-02; as of late June 2026 the nomination was still pending before the Senate HELP Committee, so Stone remains acting director.

role: Chair of the Federal Election Commission
note: FEC chair rotates annually. Broussard (Trump-nominated 2020) was elected Chair effective 2025-07-01 (Campaigns & Elections), her second time as chair (also 2021), succeeding Ellen Weintraub whom Trump moved to remove in Feb 2025. The FEC currently has only 2 of 6 commissioners (Broussard and Dara Lindenbaum) and lacks a 4-member policymaking quorum.

role: Postmaster General and Chief Executive Officer, United States Postal Service
note: 76th Postmaster General. Appointed by the USPS Board of Governors on 2025-05-09; took office 2025-07-15, succeeding Louis DeJoy (resigned March 2025) and acting PMG Doug Tulino.

role: Acting Chairman of the Consumer Product Safety Commission
note: Feldman (Vice Chairman, elected 2025-01-13) became Acting Chairman on 2025-01-21 when Alex Hoehn-Saric left the chairmanship. Trump removed the three Democratic commissioners (Hoehn-Saric, Mary Boyle, Richard Trumka Jr.) in 2025; the firings were upheld by the U.S. Supreme Court (Trump v. Boyle, July 2025). Still styled 'Acting Chairman' in 2026.

role: Chair of the National Transportation Safety Board
note: 15th NTSB Chair since August 2021. The Senate reconfirmed her in 2024 for another five-year Board term and a three-year term as Chair; she continues as Chair in 2026.

role: Chairman of the Federal Maritime Commission
note: Trump designated DiBella as Chairman on 2026-01-28. Predecessor Louis E. Sola, designated chairman 2025-01-20, ended his tenure as Chairman on 2025-06-30.

role: Chairman of the Surface Transportation Board
note: Trump designated Fuchs as Chairman effective 2025-01-20; reconfirmed to the Board in 2024 with term to 2029-01-14. Continues as Chairman in 2026.

role: Chair of the U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
note: Designated Chair by unanimous vote of the commissioners in July 2023; commissioner term ends December 2028. Vice Chair is Victoria F. Nourse. Confirmed still Chair on the live USCCR commissioners page in 2026.

role: Acting Chairman of the Merit Systems Protection Board
note: Kerner (Vice Chairman, confirmed May 2024) became Acting Chairman in February 2025 after Trump removed Chair Cathy Harris; the removal was litigated (reinstated, then re-removed). The Senate confirmed James J. Woodruff II in October 2025, restoring the Board's quorum.
